data.title: Veritex Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.02, revenue of $109.83M misses by $0.76M
data.description:
• Veritex press release (NASDAQ:VBTX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $109.83M (+2.8% Y/Y) misses by $0.76M.
  • Credit quality remained strong with a nonperforming assets (“NPAs”) to total assets ratio of 0.60% and annualized net charge-offs of 0.05% for the quarter and 0.11% year-to-date;
  • Allowance for Credit Losses (“ACL”) to total loans held-for-investment ratio (excluding mortgage warehouse (“MW”)) remained relatively unchanged at 1.28%;
  • Capital remains strong with common equity Tier 1 capital ratio of 11.05% as of June 30, 2025;
  • Book value per share increased $0.31 to $30.39 and tangible book value per share increased $0.35 to $22.68.

data.title: Trump vs. Powell: Making sense of Federal Reserve member math
data.description:
There's a lot of talk out there about whether President Trump would or could fire current Fed Chair Jerome Powell, as well as a lot of drama surrounding renovations at the Eccles Building and 1951 Constitution Avenue. It boils down to arguments of independence and competence, but the real fight is the direction of U.S. monetary policy and the impact of tariffs on the economy. These have an outsized influence on the life of everyday Americans and the electorate, making it a hot topic in the current environment. Who will succeed Powell as Fed chair?

Thought bubble: While Powell's term as board chairman expires in May 2026, he could stay on as Fed governor for another two years, which is the real worry of the Trump administration. In that role, Powell could be somewhat of a shadow Fed chair, especially with around 4 out of the remaining 6 Fed board governors subscribing to his policy views. The real reason why Trump is upping the pressure is that he wants Powell to be uncomfortable (or legally worried) about continuing in a governor spot past next year, which would give him another nomination to the board.

Should that be the case - and Trump replaces Adriana Kugler in January 2026 - he will have nominated a majority of the Fed board, including Michelle Bowman and Christopher Waller. While that would set a tone at the board of governors, whose seven members are permanent members of the Federal Open Market Committee, it won't necessarily translate entirely into monetary policy (more toward bank reserve rates). Remember that it's the FOMC's entire twelve-member committee that is responsible for setting the federal funds rate in the U.S., which influences everything from mortgage rates and car loans to credit cards and corporate balance sheets. Waller pushes for rate cut in July

Outlook: Among the five rotating FOMC members next year are notable hawks like Minneapolis' Neel Kashkari and Dallas' Lorie Logan. That could complicate the picture in achieving a consensus-driven FOMC, and could raise the stakes of internal division or weaken the predictive power of policy direction. Things could get even more turbulent if Powell decides to stay on as a Fed governor or if a FOMC chair revote doesn't take place later in the year. It could lead to two separate chair roles at the Fed board and FOMC and result in conflicting messaging at the central bank.

data.title: Schlumberger in charts: Q2 North America revenue +1%, Latin America -14% Y/Y
Chevron prevails in arbitration fight with Exxon over Guyana oilfield
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) is clear to proceed with its planned $53B acquisition of Hess (NYSE:HES) after an arbitration panel dismissed Exxon Mobil's (XOM) claim that it had a contractual right to bid for Hess' assets offshore Guyana.

The ruling handed down Friday from the International Chamber of Commerce in Paris resolved the bitter dispute between the oil giants over the prolific Stabroek Block off the coast of Guyana that holds more than 11B barrels of oil, a source of growth Chevron (NYSE:CVX) needs to help turn around its lagging performance.

Hess (NYSE:HES) +6.8%, Chevron (CVX) +2.9%, and Exxon (XOM) -0.3% pre-market.

Chevron (CVX) plans to move swiftly to close the Hess (HES) deal, which it had originally struck in October 2023.

Exxon (XOM) said it disagrees with the arbitration panel's ruling but respects the dispute resolution process.

Exxon (XOM), which operates the Stabroek block with a 45% interest, and China's CNOOC (OTCPK:CEOHF), which holds 25%, filed arbitration claims against their partner Hess (HES) last year, arguing they had a contractual right-of-first-refusal to buy Hess' 30% interest in the venture; Chevron (CVX) and Hess claimed the pre-emptive right did not apply to the sale of the entire Hess company.

The fight showed the value of the Stabroek block, which drove profits for the Exxon-led (XOM) consortium that controls all of its oil production and transformed Guyana into one of the world's fastest growing economies.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.34 beats by $0.01, revenue of $1.95B misses by $30M
data.description:
• Huntington Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:HBAN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.34 beats by $0.01.

data.title: Moderna scraps plans for messenger RNA manufacturing plant in Japan
data.description:
Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) has cancelled plans to build a messenger RNA manufacturing plant in Japan, citing concerns over the current business environment, the Japanese business publication Nikkei Asia reported on Friday.

"After careful consideration in light of the current business environment, we have decided not to proceed with this plan at this time," the newspaper reported, citing a press release from the U.S. vaccine maker’s Japanese subsidiary.

The initial development work related to the manufacturing plant started in 2023 at the Shonan Health Innovation Park in Kanagawa Prefecture.

However, the company said it has completed the construction of an R&D facility for mRNA drugs at the same location, and it would reconsider plans to develop mRNA-manufacturing sites in Japan when the business climate improves.

The decision comes as Moderna, the maker of the mRNA-based Spikevax COVID shot, is facing a declining market for COVID vaccines and struggling to compete in the market for Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) vaccines with its mRESVIA vaccine.

Additionally, President Donald Trump’s repeated threats to impose tariffs on pharmaceuticals, a product category that has largely avoided trade war due to public health impact, have also raised concerns among pharma executives.

data.title: American Express Non-GAAP EPS of $4.08 beats by $0.20, revenue of $17.86B beats by $160M
data.description:
• American Express press release (NYSE:AXP): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.08 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $17.86B (+9.4% Y/Y) beats by $160M.
  • The increase was primarily driven by increased Card Member spending, higher net interest income supported by growth in revolving loan balances, and continued strong card fee growth.
  • Consolidated provisions for credit losses were $1.4 billion, compared with $1.3 billion a year ago. The increase reflected a higher net reserve build and higher net write-offs year-over-year driven by growth in Total loans and Card Member receivables. The second-quarter net write-off rate was 2.0 percent, down from 2.1 percent a year ago.
  • Based on our strong performance year to date, we are reaffirming our full-year guidance for revenue growth of 8 to 10 percent vs. 8.14% consensus and EPS of $15.00 to $15.50 vs. $15.24 consensus

data.title: Funko amends its credit agreements in reaction to the tariff backdrop
data.description:
Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) announced that it executed an amendment to its existing credit facilities, originally dated September 17, 2021.

The amendment modifies the toy company's existing credit agreement to include waivers for the maximum net leverage ratio and the minimum fixed charge coverage ratio financial covenants for the fiscal quarters ended June 30, 2025, and ending September 30, 2025.

The amended credit agreement also adds or modifies other covenants and reduces the revolving commitments under the company’s existing credit facilities from $150 million to $135 million as of July 16, 2025, and to $125 million on December 31, 2025.

"As part of our comprehensive tariff mitigation plans, we have proactively amended our existing credit agreement to allow more covenant flexibility during this dynamic period," highlighted Funko (FNK) Chief Financial Officer Yves Le Pendeven. "We received consent to the amendment from 100% of our bank group and appreciate their continued support and partnership," added Le Pendeven.

Shares of Funko (FNK) are down 64.5% on a year-to-date basis. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on Funko (FNK) is flashing Strong Sell due to low factor grades for momentum and earnings revisions.

data.title: CES Energy Solutions renews normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• CES Energy Solutions (OTCPK:CESDF) has received approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange to implement a normal course issuer bid.
  • The new plan renews the previous bid, which ends on July 18, 2025, when the maximum number of the company’s common shares allowed under the plan will be repurchased.
  • Under the previous plan, 19.19M common shares will have been acquired through market purchases on the TSX and other alternative Canadian securities trading platforms, at a volume-weighted average purchase price of approximately $7.46 per share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: UK moves to lower voting age to 16, should US be next?
data.description:
The United Kingdom has moved to lower the voting age to 16 from 18 for all elections in the country, as part of several proposed reforms aimed at boosting political participation

The policy was part of the Labour Party's campaign in last year's election, which saw the lowest turnout since 2001. The proposed reforms are expected to secure parliamentary approval, given the party's majority in the House of Commons.

Other proposed changes include creating an automated registration system for voters, allowing bank cards as an accepted form of voter ID, and tightening political donation rules.

The proposed reform to lower the voting age would bring UK-wide elections in line with Scotland and Wales, where 16- and 17-year-olds are allowed to vote in certain local elections.

Many favor the proposal as it is legal for 16-year-olds in the UK to work full-time, which means some of them pay taxes. "If you pay in, you should have the opportunity to say what you want your money spent on, which way the government should go," said Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

But opponents say 16-year-olds are not mature enough to make political decisions independently. There are also concerns that younger voters would support liberal parties over conservatives, although this is not always the case (recall that voters under 30 swung towards Trump in 2024).

While most countries have a voting age of 18 or higher, some nations like Brazil, Austria, Cuba, and Australia have lowered it to 16 or 17. 

A third of U.S. states allow 17-year-olds to vote in primaries if they will turn 18 by the general election. According to the National Youth Rights Association, a dozen U.S. cities allow 16-year-olds to vote in school board elections or local elections.

Legislation has been reintroduced in the House of Representatives to lower the voting age to 16 for all elections, but previous attempts have not been successful.
